title: No SAST for subprocess-heavy codebase
detail: 122 subprocess calls across 31 harnesses. 0 shell=True (good), but no SAST workflow to catch regressions. Community PRs could introduce shell=True without detection.
recommendation: Add semgrep or CodeQL workflow targeting subprocess, os.system, shlex patterns.

title: 2,707 tests exist but no CI runs them
detail: Actual test count is 2,707 (1,854 unit + 740 e2e + 113 other). README badge claims 1,839 (undercount). Tests are well-structured with synthetic data. But no CI workflow enforces them -- tests are developer-honor-system only.
recommendation: Add per-harness pytest CI matrix. Unit tests need no backend, E2E tests need conditional runners.

title: Security mitigations are code-level, not CI-enforced
detail: SECURITY.md threat model is backed by real code: 0 shell=True, codec frozenset allowlists, Click.Path validation, xml_escape. But no CI gate prevents regressions. Downgraded from Quick Scan severity after verifying code quality.
recommendation: Add semgrep rules for subprocess patterns. Enable secret scanning.

title: Immature release cadence
detail: Only 1 release (v0.2.0) in 26 days. Rapid commit velocity without tagged releases makes pinning stable versions difficult.
recommendation: Establish release cadence. Per-harness versioning already exists in registry.json.

title: Bus factor risk on merge authority
detail: Primary maintainer 'Yuhao' handles most merges. 44 contributors but merge authority concentrated.
recommendation: Add 1-2 additional maintainers. Document maintainer ladder in CONTRIBUTING.md.

title: Inconsistent credential handling across harnesses
detail: AdGuardHome stores password in JSON config file. Anygen uses env vars. No shared credential abstraction or security pattern enforced across harnesses.
recommendation: Document credential handling standard in HARNESS.md. Prefer env vars over config file storage.

title: No monorepo coordination tooling
detail: 33 harnesses operate independently. No shared test runner, no CI matrix, no dependency checking across harnesses. Good for isolation but lacks cross-harness quality gates.
recommendation: Add root-level pytest runner or CI matrix. Consider a shared security-patterns library.

title: Code reuse via copy-paste, not shared library
detail: repl_skin.py is copied into each harness rather than imported from a shared package. Changes to the shared skin require updating 31+ copies.
recommendation: Publish cli-anything-core package to PyPI with shared components (repl_skin, credential helpers, security utils).

title: Missing Code of Conduct
detail: Community health at 75%. For a project with 44 contributors in 26 days, governance documentation matters.

title: Incomplete type hints, no static analysis
detail: Type hints present in some backend modules but not comprehensive. No mypy, pyright, or ruff configuration.
recommendation: Add pyproject.toml with ruff/mypy config. Start with harness entry points.

title: Viral growth outpacing governance
detail: 27,518 stars in 26 days (~1,058/day). Growth outpacing CI, security, and governance maturity. Community PRs accepted with Copilot review only.
recommendation: Prioritize CI and security tooling before adding more harnesses.

title: Subprocess safety is genuinely strong
detail: POSITIVE: 0 shell=True across 122 subprocess calls. Arguments always passed as lists. Click.Path validation for file inputs. Codec allowlists (frozenset) in media harnesses. os.path.abspath normalization. This is better than most projects of this size.
recommendation: Document this pattern in HARNESS.md as a required practice (it may already be implicit).

title: HARNESS.md is a novel, high-quality methodology
detail: POSITIVE: 7-phase SOP (analysis, design, implementation, rendering, REPL, session locking, publishing) with concrete code examples. Includes guides/ subdirectory. This is a genuine contribution to the agent tooling ecosystem -- not just a README.
recommendation: Consider publishing HARNESS.md as a standalone guide or blog post for wider adoption.

title: Chinese and Japanese READMEs frozen at 11 harnesses / 1,508 tests
detail: README_CN.md and README_JA.md last synced ~March 11. English README shows 20 harnesses and 2,005 tests. CN/JA show 11 harnesses and 1,508 tests. Missing: FreeCAD, RenderDoc, Browser, iTerm2, Novita, CloudCompare, Zotero, and no News section.
recommendation: Sync translations or add 'last updated' date with link to English README as authoritative source.

title: Hardcoded /root/ paths in plugin docs
detail: cli-anything-plugin/QUICKSTART.md and README.md contain /root/cli-anything/ hardcoded paths from dev environment (lines 38, 45, 58, 132, 171, 173). Will break for any contributor.
recommendation: Replace with relative paths or $REPO_ROOT placeholder.

title: Wrong GitHub org URL in sketch harness
detail: sketch/agent-harness/README.md links to github.com/anthropics/CLI-Anything instead of github.com/HKUDS/CLI-Anything.

title: Test infrastructure relies on sys.path hacks
detail: Every test file contains sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(...)) instead of proper package installation. 0 conftest.py files exist. tmp_dir fixture copy-pasted 30+ times.
recommendation: Add root conftest.py with shared fixtures. Use editable installs (pip install -e .) for test discovery.

title: Test documentation (TEST.md) is thorough but manual
detail: POSITIVE: 27/32 harnesses have TEST.md with class inventories and pasted pytest output. But manually maintained -- already drifting (rms docs 9 passing, has 97 functions).
recommendation: Auto-generate TEST.md from pytest runs or remove in favor of CI badges.

title: Shotcut contains outdated HARNESS.md copy
detail: shotcut/agent-harness/HARNESS.md is the pre-v0.2.0 methodology. The canonical v0.2.0 lives in cli-anything-plugin/HARNESS.md. A developer reading Shotcut's copy gets outdated guidance.
recommendation: Remove harness-local copies or symlink to canonical.
